text: The taper should maintain training intensity while reducing training volume by 60-90% to minimize accumulated fatigue without compromising adaptations.
Training frequency during a taper should be reduced by no more than 20% to preserve adaptations.
The optimal taper duration ranges between 4 and 28+ days depending on the athlete and sport.
Progressive nonlinear tapers (exponential reduction) produce greater performance benefits than step tapers (abrupt reduction).
Tapering typically improves performance by approximately 3%, with a usual range of 0.5-6.0%.
Performance gains from tapering are mediated by positive changes across cardiorespiratory, metabolic, hematological, hormonal, neuromuscular, and psychological systems.
